### Damru CLI Setup Commands

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/README.md

Run these commands for guided first-run configuration and dependency installation.

```bash
python -m damru setup
```

```bash
python -m damru install-deps
```

```bash
python -m damru install-image
```

```bash
python -m damru install-apks --download
```

```bash
python -m damru fix-wsl
```

```bash
python -m damru fix-internet
```

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --device <slug>
```

```bash
python -m damru check-env
```

```bash
python -m damru check preflight --json
```

--------------------------------

### Run Damru Setup

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/AUTOMATION_GAPS_PLAN.md

Guides configuration and can run dependency setup for Damru.

```bash
python -m damru setup
```

--------------------------------

### Setup with WSL Kernel Installation

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Automated setup for fresh Windows/WSL machines that includes installing the WSL kernel. Requires confirmation for risks.

```powershell
python -m damru setup --install-wsl-kernel -y --confirm-wsl-kernel-risk
```

--------------------------------

### Verify Damru Environment Setup

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Run Damru setup commands to install dependencies, download necessary images, and check the environment. The 'setup' command handles dependency installation and APK asset management.

```bash
python -m damru setup
python -m damru install-image
python -m damru check-env
```

--------------------------------

### Install Damru by Cloning Repository

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

For developers, clone the repository, set up a virtual environment, and install Damru in editable mode. This method also includes skipping dependency installation during setup.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/akwin1234/damru.git
cd damru
python3 -m venv venv
source venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install -U pip setuptools wheel
pip install -e .
python -m damru setup --skip-deps
```

--------------------------------

### Damru CLI Commands Overview

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

A list of common Damru CLI commands for setup, environment validation, dependency installation, system fixes, benchmarking, and device management.

```bash
python -m damru setup           # guided first-run setup and config writer
python -m damru check-env       # validate Linux/WSL dependencies and assets
python -m damru check preflight # fast read-only readiness checks for fleets
python -m damru install-deps    # install common Linux/WSL dependencies
python -m damru fix-wsl         # retry safe WSL Docker/binderfs/netfilter fixes
python -m damru fix-internet    # repair WSL/Docker/Android DNS and internet checks
python -m damru wsl-kernel status # inspect bundled/active WSL kernel state
python -m damru benchmark       # run the benchmark command
python -m damru bake-image      # bake a warm Redroid image
python -m damru devices         # list ADB devices from Linux/WSL
python -m damru force-profile   # force a named profile onto one ADB worker
python -m damru open-url        # open a URL in Android Chrome on one ADB worker
python -m damru stealth-open-url # apply Damru stealth, native-load URL, then reattach CDP for inspection
python -m damru quick-check     # run a fast local Android/Chrome sanity check
python -m damru screenshot      # capture Android display PNG through ADB
python -m damru record          # capture Android display MP4 through ADB
python -m damru view            # open optional scrcpy live viewer
python -m damru install-viewer  # check/install optional scrcpy tooling
python -m damru ui              # open the experimental local web dashboard
```

--------------------------------

### Install Viewer and Check Environment

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PROOF.md

Installs the Damru viewer and checks the environment configuration for WSL.

```powershell
python -m damru install-viewer -y
python -m damru check-env --viewer
```

--------------------------------

### Install Essential Packages on Ubuntu

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Installs necessary packages including adb, wget, curl, git, and jq for system preparation.

```bash
sudo apt update && sudo apt upgrade -y
sudo apt install adb wget curl git jq -y
```

--------------------------------

### Verified Commands for Fresh WSL Distro

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

A sequence of commands to set up, install dependencies, fix the WSL environment, install a Redroid image, perform preflight checks, start workers, and run quick checks and open a URL. This is part of a validation process.

```powershell
$env:DAMRU_REDROID_BASE_PORT = "5900"
python -m damru install-deps -y
python -m damru fix-wsl
python -m damru install-image --tar /mnt/c/path/to/damru-redroid-latest.tar
python -m damru check preflight --json --timeout 3
python -m damru ui-worker start --count 2
python -m damru quick-check --serial 127.0.0.1:5900 --output /tmp/damru-wsl-quick-5900.json
python -m damru quick-check --serial 127.0.0.1:5901 --output /tmp/damru-wsl-quick-5901.json
python -m damru open-url --serial 127.0.0.1:5900 --url https://example.com
```

--------------------------------

### Setup Damru with Sudo Password via Stdin

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Automate Damru setup on native Linux by providing the sudo password via standard input. This is useful for non-interactive scripted installations.

```bash
printf '%s\n' 'your-sudo-password' | python -m damru setup -y --sudo-password-stdin
```

--------------------------------

### Scripted Damru Setup

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Perform a scripted setup for Damru, specifying the WSL distribution and username. This is useful for automated or custom installations.

```bash
python -m damru setup -y --wsl-distro Ubuntu --wsl-username your-wsl-user
```

--------------------------------

### Install Damru Dependencies and Image

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Installs necessary dependencies and the Damru OS image. The image is auto-detected locally or downloaded if not found.

```bash
python -m damru install-deps -y
python -m damru install-image
```

--------------------------------

### Set Up Damru Development Environment

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md

Clone the repository, set up a virtual environment, install dependencies, and check the environment configuration.

```bash
git clone https://github.com/akwin1234/damru.git
cd damru
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
pip install -e .
python -m damru install-deps -y
python -m damru check-env
```

--------------------------------

### Install and Verify Viewer Tooling

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Installs and checks the environment for Damru's viewer capabilities. Ensure this is run before attempting to use viewer features.

```bash
python -m damru install-viewer
python -m damru check-env --viewer
```

--------------------------------

### Recommended Damru Installation Flow

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/IMAGE_AUTOPULL_FIX.md

Use this sequence for installing dependencies, the baked Redroid image, and performing initial checks.

```bash
python -m damru install-deps -y
python -m damru install-image
python -m damru check preflight
python -m damru check-env
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies with Sudo Password via Stdin

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Automate dependency installation for Damru on native Linux by providing the sudo password via standard input. This is useful for non-interactive scripted installations.

```bash
printf '%s\n' 'your-sudo-password' | python -m damru install-deps -y --sudo-password-stdin
```

--------------------------------

### Install Docker Engine

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Downloads and installs the Docker engine using the official installation script. It also adds the current user to the docker group.

```bash
curl -fsSL https://get.docker.com -o get-docker.sh
sudo sh get-docker.sh
sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
```

--------------------------------

### Install Viewer Components

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/VIEWER.md

Installs necessary viewer components, such as scrcpy, for Damru. The command may vary depending on the operating system.

```bash
python -m damru install-viewer
```

--------------------------------

### Start Custom WebView App

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Starts a custom Android WebView application. This command is used after applying profile hardening to test the embedded WebView.

```bash
adb -s 127.0.0.1:5600 shell am start -n com.example.webview/.MainActivity -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d https://example.com
```

--------------------------------

### Install Damru Dependencies and Image

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Installs Damru's dependencies, loads the Redroid Docker image, and checks the environment. These commands are idempotent.

```bash
python -m damru install-deps
python -m damru install-image
python -m damru check-env
```

--------------------------------

### Launch Damru UI

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/AUTOMATION_GAPS_PLAN.md

Starts the Damru User Interface. Note that the UI is currently marked as experimental.

```bash
python -m damru ui
```

--------------------------------

### Install Damru Dependencies and Check Environment on Linux

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PROOF.md

Installs Damru dependencies and checks the environment configuration for native Linux.

```bash
/tmp/damru-linux-proof/venv/bin/python -m damru install-deps -y
/tmp/damru-linux-proof/venv/bin/python -m damru check-env --viewer
```

--------------------------------

### Start Redroid Workers

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Starts a specified number of Redroid workers. This is part of the setup and testing process.

```bash
python -m damru ui-worker start --count 2
```

--------------------------------

### Damru Install Image with Download Option

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Use this command to install a Damru image, with an option to download the tarball if it's not locally available.

```bash
python -m damru install-image --download
```

--------------------------------

### Perform Deep Environment Check with Viewer

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/AUTOMATION_GAPS_PLAN.md

Validates environment setup, including optional viewer checks. This check is slower than preflight.

```bash
python -m damru check-env --viewer
```

--------------------------------

### Install Damru on Windows with Pip

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Use this command to upgrade packaging tools before installing Damru if you encounter installation errors on older Windows Python versions.

```powershell
python -m pip install -U pip setuptools wheel
pip install git+https://github.com/akwin1234/damru.git
```

--------------------------------

### Start Custom WebView Activity

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/chrome-apks/README.md

Initiate an Android activity for a custom WebView application. This command is used after applying Android-level hardening to test the embedded WebView.

```bash
adb -s 127.0.0.1:5600 shell am start -n com.example.webview/.MainActivity -d https://example.com
```

--------------------------------

### Preflight Check and UI Worker Start on Linux

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PROOF.md

Performs a preflight check and starts UI workers for Damru on a native Linux system.

```bash
python -m damru check preflight --json --timeout 3
python -m damru ui-worker start --count 2
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Docker Daemon for Single Worker Setup

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Starts the Docker daemon with specific flags to disable iptables, ip6tables, and bridge networking, suitable for a single-worker Redroid setup on WSL.

```bash
dockerd \
  --iptables=false \
  --ip6tables=false \
  --bridge=none \
  --host=unix:///var/run/docker.sock
```

--------------------------------

### Install Binder Modules in Linux VM

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Installs extra kernel modules and loads binder devices, typically required for Android-specific functionalities within a Linux environment.

```bash
apt install linux-modules-extra-$(uname -r)
modprobe binder_linux devices="binder,hwbinder,vndbinder"
```

--------------------------------

### Open Live Viewer

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/VIEWER.md

Starts the live viewer for a specified device, which uses scrcpy to display and control the Android device. This is useful for debugging and manual inspection.

```bash
python -m damru view --serial wsl:127.0.0.1:5600
```

--------------------------------

### Start UI Workers and Perform Quick Checks

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PROOF.md

Starts Damru UI workers and performs quick checks on specified serial ports for WSL.

```powershell
python -m damru ui-worker start --count 2
python -m damru quick-check --serial 127.0.0.1:5900 --output /tmp/damru-wsl-quick-5900.json
python -m damru quick-check --serial 127.0.0.1:5901 --output /tmp/damru-wsl-quick-5901.json
```

--------------------------------

### Install Dependencies and Fix WSL

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PROOF.md

Installs Damru dependencies and applies fixes specific to the WSL environment.

```powershell
python -m damru install-deps -y
python -m damru fix-wsl
```

--------------------------------

### Install Damru in Editable Mode with Development Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PROOF.md

Installs Damru in editable mode, including development dependencies, within a virtual environment.

```bash
/tmp/damru-linux-proof/venv/bin/python -m pip install -e .[dev]
```

--------------------------------

### Damru CLI Preflight and Environment Checks

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Commands to run fast readiness checks and verify the environment setup for Damru. These are useful before running more intensive operations.

```bash
python -m damru check preflight
python -m damru check-env
python -m damru quick-check --serial 127.0.0.1:5600
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Network and Info Checks

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Verify Docker installation and network capabilities by creating a test bridge network and inspecting Docker daemon information.

```bash
docker info
docker network create bridge test
docker run --rm -p 5600:5555 ...
```

--------------------------------

### Install Bundled WSL Kernel

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Installs the Damru bundled WSL kernel. Requires confirmation for risks and a WSL shutdown to apply changes. Followed by environment checks.

```powershell
python -m damru wsl-kernel install --yes --confirm-wsl-kernel-risk
wsl --shutdown
python -m damru fix-wsl
python -m damru check-env --viewer
```

--------------------------------

### Install Damru Dependencies

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/AUTOMATION_GAPS_PLAN.md

Installs Linux/WSL packages, Docker, ADB, binderfs tools, and applies Damru's Playwright patch. The -y flag assumes yes to all prompts.

```bash
python -m damru install-deps -y
```

--------------------------------

### WSL2 Kernel Module Configuration

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Example of how to configure WSL2 to load custom kernel modules using the .wslconfig file.

```ini
[wsl2]
kernelModules=C:\Program Files\WSL\tools\modules.vhd
loadDefaultKernelModules=true
```

--------------------------------

### Start Damru Container

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Run the Damru Android container with specified volume mounts, port mappings, and Android boot parameters. This command starts the container in detached mode and makes it privileged.

```bash
docker run -itd --rm --privileged \
    -v ~/data:/data \
    -p 5555:5555 \
    damru-redroid:latest \
    androidboot.redroid_width=1080 \
    androidboot.redroid_height=2400 \
    androidboot.redroid_dpi=480
```

--------------------------------

### Example Python Script

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_FALLBACK_TEST_RESULTS.md

This is the final full run script for the legacy WSL fallback test. It passed all tests, indicating successful execution of the Damru functionalities within the specified environment.

```python
import damru

# This is a placeholder for the actual example.py content.
# The original document only mentions that 'example.py' passed tests.
# No actual code for example.py was provided in the source text.
print("example.py executed successfully.")

```

--------------------------------

### Docker Startup with Disabled Features

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Starting Docker with specific flags to bypass bridge and IP masquerading, which may still fail on limited kernels.

```text
Failed to create bridge docker0 via netlink: operation not supported
```

--------------------------------

### Install WSL Kernel with Fix-WSL

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Installs the bundled WSL kernel using the 'fix-wsl' command, useful if normal repair fails. Requires WSL shutdown to apply.

```powershell
python -m damru fix-wsl --install-kernel --yes --confirm-wsl-kernel-risk
wsl --shutdown
```

--------------------------------

### Install Damru via Pip

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Install Damru into a Python virtual environment using pip. This is the recommended method for modern Ubuntu systems to avoid conflicts with system Python.

```bash
sudo apt install -y python3-venv
python3 -m venv .venv
source .venv/bin/activate
python -m pip install -U pip setuptools wheel
pip install git+https://github.com/akwin1234/damru.git
```

--------------------------------

### Damru WSL Kernel Installer Configuration

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Configuration settings applied by the Damru WSL kernel installer, including networking and DNS settings.

```text
dnsTunneling=true
networkingMode=NAT
```

--------------------------------

### Install Damru Baked Image

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/AUTOMATION_GAPS_PLAN.md

Loads or downloads the 'damru-redroid:latest' image when available.

```bash
python -m damru install-image
```

--------------------------------

### Example APK Asset Bundle Structure

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Illustrates the expected directory layout for the extracted APK asset bundle. This structure is used by Damru to manage Chrome, WebView, and TTS assets.

```text
chrome-apks/
  148.0.7778.178/
    base.apk
    split_chrome.apk
    google_trichrome_library.apk
    webview.apk
    vanadium_trichrome_library.apk
  google_tts.apk
  espeak.apk
  rhvoice.apk
  magisk.apk
```

--------------------------------

### Check Docker Information and Network List

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Retrieves detailed information about the Docker installation and lists all Docker networks, useful for verifying Docker's operational status.

```bash
sudo docker info
sudo docker network ls
```

--------------------------------

### Open Live Viewer Without Control

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/VIEWER.md

Starts the live viewer for a specified device but disables user control (keyboard, mouse, touch events). Use this when you only need to watch the device.

```bash
python -m damru view --serial wsl:127.0.0.1:5600 --no-control
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Daemon with Nftables Backend Failure

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Attempting to start the Docker daemon with the nftables backend on WSL2 results in errors related to missing NAT table rules and the 'xt_addrtype' module.

```bash
dockerd --host=unix:///var/run/docker.sock
```

--------------------------------

### Check Damru Environment

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/UI.md

Use the Damru CLI to perform an environment check. This is useful for verifying the setup and configuration of Damru.

```bash
python -m damru check-env
```

--------------------------------

### Check WSL Version and Networking Status

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Retrieve detailed information about the installed WSL version, its current status, and registered distributions.

```powershell
wsl --version
wsl --status
wsl -l -v
```

--------------------------------

### Raw/Unbaked Damru Flow for Baking and Debugging

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/IMAGE_AUTOPULL_FIX.md

This flow is for advanced use cases like baking custom images, debugging, or APK recovery. It involves installing APKs, baking an image, saving it, and then installing the image.

```bash
python -m damru install-apks --download
python -m damru bake-image --image damru-redroid:latest
docker save damru-redroid:latest -o damru-redroid-latest.tar
sha256sum damru-redroid-latest.tar > damru-redroid-latest.tar.sha256
python -m damru install-image --tar damru-redroid-latest.tar
python -m damru quick-check --serial 127.0.0.1:5600
```

--------------------------------

### WSL2 Configuration for Damru

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Configure WSL distribution and username for Damru's Linux setup. The password field is kept for compatibility but is not actively used with the current `wsl -u root` approach.

```python
# damru/config.py
WSL_DISTRO = "Ubuntu"
WSL_USERNAME = "your-wsl-user"
WSL_PASSWORD = ""  # Kept for compatibility; current WSL setup uses wsl -u root
```

--------------------------------

### Run Fleet Preflight Checks

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Execute read-only readiness checks across multiple hosts before starting Redroid workers. Use --json for tooling, --strict for CI failures on warnings, and --no-adb to skip ADB checks.

```bash
python -m damru check preflight
```

```bash
python -m damru check preflight --json
```

```bash
python -m damru check preflight --strict
```

```bash
python -m damru check preflight --no-adb
```

--------------------------------

### Sanitized Proof Metadata Example

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PROOF.md

This JSON object represents sanitized metadata captured during a proof of concept run. It includes details about the browser environment, user agent, hardware specifications, and proxy information.

```json
{
  "example_title": "Example Domain",
  "android_user_agent": true,
  "hardwareConcurrency": 8,
  "maxTouchPoints": 5,
  "browser_timezone": "America/New_York",
  "proxy_country": "US",
  "proxy_timezone": "America/New_York"
}
```

--------------------------------

### Install Linux Kernel Modules for Redroid

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Installs necessary Linux kernel modules on a Debian/Ubuntu system for Redroid functionality, including binder and netfilter support. Ensure your kernel is up-to-date.

```bash
sudo apt update
sudo apt install -y linux-modules-extra-$(uname -r)

# Android kernel bits for Redroid
sudo modprobe binder_linux devices="binder,hwbinder,vndbinder"
# ashmem if needed and available:
# sudo modprobe ashmem_linux

# Sanity check netfilter
sudo iptables -t filter -L
sudo iptables -t nat -L
```

--------------------------------

### Shutdown WSL

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/damru/wsl_kernel/README.md

The user must restart WSL after installing or changing a WSL kernel. This command shuts down all running WSL instances.

```powershell
wsl --shutdown
```

--------------------------------

### Create and Activate Python Virtual Environment

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PROOF.md

Sets up a Python virtual environment for Damru on a native Linux system.

```bash
python3 -m venv /tmp/damru-linux-proof/venv
/tmp/damru-linux-proof/venv/bin/python -m pip install -U pip setuptools wheel
```

--------------------------------

### Define WSL Kernel Module Path

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Explicitly define the path to the stock Windows WSL installation's kernel modules if automatic loading fails. This is a configuration setting for WSL.

```ini
kernelModules=C:\\Program Files\\WSL\\tools\\modules.vhd
```

--------------------------------

### Basic Async Usage with Damru

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Demonstrates standard asynchronous usage of Damru to launch a browser, navigate to a website, and take a screenshot. Use 'random' for device to pick from available profiles. Leave timezone/locale unset to follow the active proxy exit.

```python
import asyncio
from damru import AsyncDamru

async def main():
    print("Launching Damru...")
    
# device="random" picks from 155 real Android device profiles.
    # Leave timezone/locale unset so Damru follows the active proxy exit.
    async with AsyncDamru(
        device="random", 
        proxy="socks5://your.proxy.ip:1080",
        debug=True
    ) as browser:
        
        # 'browser' is a standard Playwright BrowserContext!
        page = await browser.new_page()
        
        print("Navigating to CreepJS to test stealth...")
        await page.goto("https://abrahamjuliot.github.io/creepjs/")
        await page.wait_for_timeout(10000)
        await page.screenshot(path="creepjs_score.png")
        print("Done! Check creepjs_score.png")

asyncio.run(main())
```

--------------------------------

### Setting Chrome APK Path Manually

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Manually set the CHROME_APK environment variable to a specific Chrome split-APK version directory if automatic detection fails. This example shows a Linux/WSL path.

```bash
export CHROME_APK=/home/damru/chrome-apks/148.0.7778.178
```

--------------------------------

### Run Multiple Redroid Containers with Bridge Networking

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Creates a Docker network and runs two Redroid containers, each mapped to a distinct host port. This setup is suitable for multi-worker Redroid instances on a Linux VM or native Linux.

```bash
sudo docker network create redroid-net

sudo docker run -d --privileged \
  --network redroid-net \
  -p 5555:5555 \
  --name redroid1 \
  redroid/redroid:14.0.0_64only-latest

sudo docker run -d --privileged \
  --network redroid-net \
  -p 5556:5555 \
  --name redroid2 \
  redroid/redroid:14.0.0_64only-latest
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Daemon with Host Network Fallback

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

This configuration starts the Docker daemon with iptables and bridge networking disabled, falling back to host networking. It's a workaround for environments where standard Docker networking fails.

```bash
dockerd --iptables=false --ip6tables=false --bridge=none --host=unix:///var/run/docker.sock
```

--------------------------------

### Force WebRTC IP Handling Policy

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Inject Chromium command-line flags via WebDriver to enforce WebRTC IP handling policy, ensuring IP security regardless of the host kernel's firewall state. This is a fallback for compromised WSL setups.

```python
from selenium import webdriver

options = webdriver.ChromeOptions()
options.add_argument("--force-webrtc-ip-handling-policy=disable_non_proxied_udp")

driver = webdriver.Chrome(options=options)
```

--------------------------------

### Force Damru Profile on ADB Worker

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Use 'force-profile' to assign a specific Android identity to a running worker before attaching a harness. Examples show basic usage and options like --no-chrome and --clear-proxy.

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device xiaomi_redmi_9a
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device motorola_moto_g_5s_plus --no-chrome --clear-proxy
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device xiaomi_redmi_9a --browser-package org.chromium.webview_shell --locale pt-BR --timezone America/Sao_Paulo
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Redroid ADB Port Binding via Environment Variable

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Modify the ADB port binding for Redroid containers by setting the persist.adb.tcp.port property using an environment variable during container startup. This helps avoid socket binding collisions in multi-container setups.

```bash
-e REDROID_PROP_persist.adb.tcp.port=5556
```

--------------------------------

### Check Preflight Configuration

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/tests/README.md

Perform a preflight check of the environment configuration, outputting results in JSON format and disabling ADB.

```bash
python -m damru check preflight --json --no-adb
```

--------------------------------

### Run Live Environment Probes

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/tests/README.md

Execute environment-heavy probes that require a Linux/WSL Redroid environment, Docker, ADB, and live websites.

```bash
python -m pytest --run-damru-probes -q
```

--------------------------------

### Damru CLI Benchmark Tests

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Commands to run Damru's benchmark suite for proof and stealth checks. Allows specifying a device, proxy, and specific tests.

```bash
# Run all benchmark tests on a random device
python -m damru benchmark --device random

# Run specific tests with a proxy
python -m damru benchmark --device samsung_galaxy_s24_ultra --proxy socks5://ip:port --tests creepjs cloudflare
```

--------------------------------

### Load Custom OS Image (WSL2)

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Load a pre-baked Docker image for Damru within a WSL2 environment. Ensure the tarball is accessible and verify its integrity before loading.

```bash
sha256sum -c damru-redroid-latest.tar.sha256
docker load -i damru-redroid-latest.tar
```

--------------------------------

### Preflight Readiness Check with JSON Output

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/IMAGE_AUTOPULL_FIX.md

Perform a fast, read-only preflight check of the environment. Use the --json flag for automation and specify a timeout.

```bash
python -m damru check preflight --json --timeout 3
```

--------------------------------

### Perform Quick Checks and Open URL on Linux

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PROOF.md

Executes quick checks and opens a URL on specified serial ports for Damru on Linux.

```bash
python -m damru quick-check --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --output /tmp/damru-vps-quick-5600.json
python -m damru quick-check --serial 127.0.0.1:5601 --output /tmp/damru-vps-quick-5601.json
python -m damru open-url --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --url https://example.com
```

--------------------------------

### Build Damru Redroid Docker Image (CLI)

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/scripts/README.md

The command-line interface equivalent for building the Damru Redroid Docker image.

```bash
python -m damru bake-image --image damru-redroid:latest
```

--------------------------------

### Capture Proof Screenshots

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/scripts/README.md

Captures sanitized viewport screenshots for proof targets. Runtime proxy values are read from environment variables only.

```bash
DAMRU_PROXY='socks5://user:pass@host:port' \
DAMRU_HTTP_PROXY='172.17.0.1:18888' \
python scripts/capture_proof.py --device pixel_8_pro --out docs/assets/proof/sites
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize AsyncDamru with a Specific Device Profile

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/DEVICE_PROFILES.md

Instantiate `AsyncDamru` using a device's exact name or slug. This is useful for targeting specific device configurations in your tests.

```python
async with AsyncDamru(device="Samsung Galaxy S24 Ultra") as browser:
    ...
```

```python
async with AsyncDamru(device="samsung_galaxy_s24_ultra") as browser:
    ...
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize AsyncDamru with Chrome APK

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Initialize the AsyncDamru client with the path to the Chrome APK. This allows Damru to use the specified APK for browser operations.

```python
from damru import AsyncDamru

async with AsyncDamru(chrome_apk="/home/damru/chrome-apks/145.0.7632.75") as browser:
    page = await browser.new_page()
```

--------------------------------

### Run Damru Benchmark Suite

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Execute the built-in benchmark suite to verify Damru's stealth capabilities against anti-bot systems. This command helps reproduce benchmark results.

```python
python -m damru benchmark
```

--------------------------------

### Async DamruPool Usage

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Demonstrates how to initialize and use the asynchronous `DamruPool` in 'auto' mode for managing Docker containers. This is suitable for massive parallelization tasks.

```python
from damru import DamruPool

# mode="auto" automatically starts and manages Redroid Docker containers
async with DamruPool(mode="auto", max_devices=10) as pool:
    # pool.session() provides an AsyncDamru context from an available container
    async with pool.session() as browser:
        page = await browser.new_page()
        ...
```

--------------------------------

### Run Damru Preflight Check

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/PREFLIGHT_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.txt

Execute the basic preflight check command for Damru.

```bash
python -m damru check preflight
```

--------------------------------

### Run Fast Unit Tests

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/tests/README.md

Execute only the fast unit tests without requiring external environments like Docker or ADB.

```bash
python -m pytest -q
```

--------------------------------

### Force Profile for WebView Shell

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/AUTOMATION_GAPS_PLAN.md

Writes WebView command-line/preferences and applies native memory preload for WebView harness validation. Specify the serial, device type, and browser package.

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device pixel_8_pro --browser-package org.chromium.webview_shell
```

--------------------------------

### Run Damru Preflight Check with JSON Output

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/PREFLIGHT_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.txt

Execute the preflight check and output results in JSON format.

```bash
python -m damru check preflight --json
```

--------------------------------

### Check Legacy Linux/WSL iptables

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Displays the initialized NAT and filter chains, verifying legacy iptables configuration.

```bash
sudo iptables -t nat -L
```

```bash
sudo iptables -t filter -L
```

--------------------------------

### Build Damru Redroid Docker Image

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/scripts/README.md

Builds a warm `damru-redroid:latest` Docker image. Use this script inside Linux or WSL2.

```bash
python scripts/bake_image.py --image-name damru-redroid:latest
docker save damru-redroid:latest -o damru-redroid-latest.tar
sha256sum damru-redroid-latest.tar > damru-redroid-latest.tar.sha256
```

--------------------------------

### Setting Chrome APK Path on Windows (WSL)

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Manually set the CHROME_APK environment variable on Windows by using the WSL path to the extracted Chrome split-APK version directory.

```bash
export CHROME_APK=/mnt/c/Users/you/Downloads/damru/chrome-apks/148.0.7778.178
```

--------------------------------

### Run Alpine Container with Port Publishing

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Launches an Alpine Linux container and exposes a port, testing Docker's port publishing functionality which relies on NAT.

```bash
sudo docker run --rm -p 5600:5555 alpine:latest sleep 60
```

--------------------------------

### List and Check Loaded Network Modules

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Lists available kernel modules and checks if specific networking modules like iptables, NAT, and bridge are loaded.

```bash
ls -R /lib/modules/"$(uname -r)"
lsmod | egrep 'iptable|ip_tables|nf_nat|xt_addrtype|br_netfilter|bridge'
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize AsyncDamru Context

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Use AsyncDamru as an asynchronous context manager to orchestrate stealth layers and obtain a Playwright BrowserContext. Requires specifying a device and optionally a proxy.

```python
from damru import AsyncDamru

async with AsyncDamru(device="pixel_8_pro", proxy="socks5://...") as context:
    page = await context.new_page()
    await page.goto("https://creepjs.com")
```

--------------------------------

### Bake Custom Damru OS Image

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Rebuilds the Damru OS image from source and saves it as a Docker tarball. Includes generating the SHA-256 checksum.

```bash
python -m damru bake-image --image damru-redroid:latest
docker save damru-redroid:latest -o damru-redroid-latest.tar
sha256sum damru-redroid-latest.tar > damru-redroid-latest.tar.sha256
```

--------------------------------

### Run Browsers Benchmark with Damru

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/BROWSERS_BENCHMARK_REPORT.md

This script executes the external benchmark using the Damru Redroid adapter. Ensure the DAMRU_BENCH_DEVICE and DAMRU_REPO environment variables are set, and a valid residential proxy is configured in DAMRU_BENCH_PROXY for proxy-mode testing.

```powershell
$env:DAMRU_BENCH_DEVICE='Samsung Galaxy S23'
$env:DAMRU_REPO='C:\path\to\damru'
python C:\path\to\damru\scripts\run_browsers_benchmark_damru.py
```

--------------------------------

### Manually Load Linux/WSL Kernel Modules

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Tests the kmod subsystem by manually loading required Netfilter modules and checks for silent success.

```bash
sudo modprobe xt_addrtype && sudo modprobe ip_tables && sudo modprobe iptable_nat && sudo modprobe iptable_filter && sudo modprobe br_netfilter
```

--------------------------------

### Accessing Android Device Profiles

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Shows how to retrieve information about specific Android device profiles using `get_device` and how to select a random profile with specific criteria using `get_random_device`.

```python
from damru import list_device_names, get_device, get_devices_by_tier, get_random_device

# Get hardware specs for a specific phone
pixel = get_device("pixel_8_pro")
print(f"Cores: {pixel.hardware_concurrency}, RAM: {pixel.device_memory}GB")

# Select a random Android 13 profile
random_phone = get_random_device(android_version="13")

```

--------------------------------

### List Available Devices

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/VIEWER.md

Lists all available Damru devices. This command helps in identifying the serial identifiers for connecting to specific workers.

```bash
python -m damru devices
```

--------------------------------

### Run Browsers Benchmark with Damru

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Adapter script to run the techinz/browsers-benchmark against Damru. This script is used to generate external benchmark reports.

```python
scripts/run_browsers_benchmark_damru.py
```

--------------------------------

### List Windows WSL Distributions

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Verifies that the target distribution (e.g., Ubuntu-26.04) is running on WSL version 2.

```powershell
wsl -l -v
```

--------------------------------

### Manual Linux/WSL APK Extraction

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Manually extract the Chrome APK bundle on Linux or WSL. Ensure the target directory exists and has the correct ownership.

```bash
sudo mkdir -p /home/damru && sudo chown "$USER:$USER" /home/damru && unzip chrome-apks.zip -d /home/damru/chrome-apks
```

--------------------------------

### Run Damru CLI Commands

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Execute various Damru commands from the command line for device management, screenshots, recording, and opening URLs.

```bash
python -m damru devices
```

```bash
python -m damru screenshot --serial wsl:127.0.0.1:5600 --output screen.png
```

```bash
python -m damru record --serial wsl:127.0.0.1:5600 --time-limit 30 --output clip.mp4
```

```bash
python -m damru stealth-open-url --serial wsl:127.0.0.1:5600 --url https://example.com
```

```bash
python -m damru view --serial wsl:127.0.0.1:5600 --no-control
```

--------------------------------

### Check Nftables Ruleset

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Displays the current ruleset for nftables, the successor to iptables, if it is being used.

```bash
sudo nft list ruleset
```

--------------------------------

### Run Damru Unit Tests

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PROOF.md

Executes the Damru unit test suite on a native Linux system.

```bash
/tmp/damru-linux-proof/venv/bin/python -m pytest -q
```

--------------------------------

### Load iptables Modules on Standard Linux

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

This command attempts to load necessary iptables modules on a standard Linux distribution. It is provided for comparison, as these modules are typically unavailable in stock WSL2 kernels.

```bash
sudo modprobe ip_tables iptable_nat iptable_filter xt_addrtype br_netfilter
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize Synchronous Damru Context

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Use the synchronous Damru class as a context manager for traditional scripts or multi-threaded environments. Methods and parameters mirror AsyncDamru but without the 'await' keyword.

```python
from damru import Damru

with Damru(device="pixel_7") as context:
    page = context.new_page()
    page.goto("https://bot.sannysoft.com")
```

--------------------------------

### Download Raw APK Assets

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/AUTOMATION_GAPS_PLAN.md

Downloads and extracts Chrome/WebView/TTS/resetprop assets to '/home/damru/chrome-apks'.

```bash
python -m damru install-apks --download
```

--------------------------------

### Run Specific Test Files

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/tests/README.md

Execute specific test files or modules, such as preflight CLI tests or image unit tests.

```bash
python -m pytest tests/test_preflight_cli.py -q
```

```bash
python -m pytest tests/test_images_unit.py tests/test_root_webrtc.py -q
```

--------------------------------

### Check nftables Stack

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Verifies the current state of the modern nftables routing table configuration.

```bash
nft list ruleset
```

--------------------------------

### Custom WebView App Profile with Proxy

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Apply Android-level profile hardening to a custom WebView app and launch it. This ensures the system WebView provider is aligned and applies necessary configurations without directly targeting Chrome.

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device pixel_8_pro --no-chrome --proxy socks5://user:pass@host:port
adb -s 127.0.0.1:5600 shell am start -n com.example.webview/.MainActivity -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d https://example.com
```

--------------------------------

### Run Damru Preflight Check in Strict Mode

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/PREFLIGHT_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.txt

Execute the preflight check with strict validation rules, converting warnings to failures.

```bash
python -m damru check preflight --strict
```

--------------------------------

### WebView Shell Profile with Proxy

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Configure a WebView Shell with a specific proxy. This command applies profile hardening, patches preferences, and enables WebRTC blocking when a proxy is active.

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device pixel_8_pro --browser-package org.chromium.webview_shell --proxy socks5://user:pass@host:port
adb -s 127.0.0.1:5600 shell am start -n org.chromium.webview_shell/.WebViewBrowserActivity -a android.intent.action.VIEW -d https://example.com
```

--------------------------------

### Damru WSL Worker Network Mapping

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Illustrates how Damru maps WSL worker instances to specific network ports for ADB access.

```text
worker 0 -> wsl:127.0.0.1:5600
worker 1 -> wsl:127.0.0.1:5601
worker N -> wsl:127.0.0.1:(5600 + N)
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Linux/WSL Module Availability

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Checks if the stock modules.vhdx is mounted by listing kernel and net module directories.

```bash
ls /lib/modules/$(uname -r)
```

--------------------------------

### Connection Pooling with DamruPoolSync

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Demonstrates scaling scraping operations using DamruPoolSync for concurrent execution across multiple Docker containers. Proxies can be provided to the pool.

```python
from damru import DamruPoolSync

proxies = [
    "socks5://proxy1:1080",
    "socks5://proxy2:1080",
    "socks5://proxy3:1080"
]

with DamruPoolSync(mode="auto", max_devices=3, proxies=proxies) as pool:
    for i in range(3):
        with pool.session() as context:
            page = context.new_page()
            page.goto("https://example.com/api/scrape_target")
            print(f"Worker {i} finished scraping: {page.title()}")
```

--------------------------------

### Damru Pool and Redroid Settings

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Configure the operating mode for device management, the number of concurrent devices, and the Redroid Docker image to use.

```python
MODE = "auto"          # "auto" = manages Docker containers; "mumu" = local VMs; "manual" = ADB
NUM_DEVICES = 10       # How many concurrent containers to spin up/maintain
REDROID_IMAGE = "damru-redroid:latest"  # The Docker image to use
```

--------------------------------

### Check Damru Preflight

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Performs read-only checks for Docker, ADB, binderfs, image, APK, resource, WSL kernel, port, and config. Outputs results in JSON format, suitable for fleet scripts.

```python
python -m damru check preflight
```

--------------------------------

### Damru Configuration: Custom WSL2 Settings

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Configure custom WSL2 settings for Damru before initialization. This includes specifying the WSL distribution and username.

```python
import damru.config

# Custom WSL2 settings
damru.config.WSL_DISTRO = "Ubuntu-22.04"
damru.config.WSL_USERNAME = "my-wsl-user"
damru.config.WSL_PASSWORD = ""  # compatibility only; setup asks for sudo when needed
```

--------------------------------

### Run Damru Preflight Check with Custom Timeout

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/PREFLIGHT_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.txt

Execute the preflight check with a specified timeout duration in seconds.

```bash
python -m damru check preflight --timeout N
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Android Profile to WebView Shell

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/README.md

Applies a Damru Android profile with WebView-specific hardening for harness validation. Use `--no-chrome` for custom apps embedding system WebView.

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --browser-package org.chromium.webview_shell
```

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --no-chrome
```

--------------------------------

### Attempting to Load Kernel Modules

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

These commands attempt to load necessary kernel modules for iptables and bridging functionality, which fail on the stock WSL2 kernel.

```bash
modprobe xt_addrtype
modprobe ip_tables
modprobe iptable_nat
modprobe br_netfilter
```

--------------------------------

### Check WSL Kernel Modules

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Verify the availability of essential kernel modules for networking and iptables functionality within WSL.

```bash
uname -a
zcat /proc/config.gz
ls /lib/modules/$(uname -r)
lsmod
modprobe xt_addrtype
modprobe ip_tables
modprobe iptable_nat
modprobe iptable_filter
modprobe br_netfilter
```

--------------------------------

### Explicit Chrome Pinning with Version Matching

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/IMAGE_AUTOPULL_FIX.md

This command forces a specific Chrome version profile. It only succeeds if the specified version folder contains a matching webview.apk or TrichromeWebView.apk.

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device pixel_8_pro --rotate-chrome --chrome-version 148.0.7778.217
```

--------------------------------

### Capture Full Android Display

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Captures the full Android display as a PNG image or a video clip with a specified time limit.

```bash
python -m damru screenshot --serial wsl:127.0.0.1:5600 --output screen.png
python -m damru record --serial wsl:127.0.0.1:5600 --time-limit 30 --output clip.mp4
```

--------------------------------

### Configure WSL2 Kernel Module Loading

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Enables loading of default kernel modules in WSL2 by modifying the .wslconfig file.

```ini
[wsl2]
loadDefaultKernelModules=true
```

--------------------------------

### Revert to Legacy iptables API on Ubuntu 26.04

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Force Ubuntu 26.04 userspace to use the legacy iptables API to resolve Docker's bridge driver initialization issues caused by the nftables backend. Ensure necessary modules are exposed before executing.

```bash
sudo update-alternatives --set iptables /usr/sbin/iptables-legacy
sudo update-alternatives --set ip6tables /usr/sbin/ip6tables-legacy
```

--------------------------------

### Test Docker Bridge Network Creation

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Creates a Docker bridge network to ensure it generates without iptables errors.

```bash
docker network create bridge test_net
```

--------------------------------

### SOCKS5 to HTTP Proxy Bridge

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/scripts/README.md

Provides a local HTTP CONNECT bridge for proof runs. It handles Android needing an unauthenticated HTTP proxy when the upstream proxy is authenticated SOCKS5. Credentials are read from the runtime environment.

```bash
UPSTREAM_PROXY='socks5://user:pass@host:port' \
python scripts/socks_http_bridge.py --listen 0.0.0.0 --port 18888
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Linux/WSL Kernel Configuration

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Confirms that essential Netfilter modules (NF_NAT, ADDRTYPE) are built and Binder is enabled.

```bash
zcat /proc/config.gz | grep -E "NF_NAT|ADDRTYPE|BINDER"
```

--------------------------------

### Local Artifacts Directory Placeholder

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_FALLBACK_TEST_RESULTS.md

This indicates the location where local artifacts from the test run were written. The actual directory path is dynamic and represented by a placeholder.

```text
<local-artifacts-dir>
```

--------------------------------

### Create Docker Bridge Network

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Attempts to create a Docker bridge network, which can fail if the necessary kernel modules or configurations are missing.

```bash
sudo docker network create test-bridge
```

--------------------------------

### Check Environment and Bake Image

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Checks the Damru environment and bakes a Redroid image using the detected APK asset bundle. This is a common workflow after manual extraction.

```bash
python -m damru check-env
python -m damru bake-image --image damru-redroid:latest
```

--------------------------------

### Run Damru Preflight Check without ADB

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/PREFLIGHT_IMPLEMENTATION_PLAN.txt

Execute the preflight check, disabling ADB checks.

```bash
python -m damru check preflight --no-adb
```

--------------------------------

### Proxy, Timezone, and Locale Configuration

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Set optional proxy, timezone, and locale values. When unset (None), Damru automatically determines these based on the proxy exit node for consistent session behavior.

```python
PROXY = None        # Optional: SOCKS5/HTTP proxy URL for Python-side checks
HTTP_PROXY = None   # Optional: Android system HTTP proxy as host:port
TIMEZONE = None     # Auto from proxy exit when unset
LOCALE = None       # Auto from proxy country when unset
```

--------------------------------

### Docker IPvlan Network Creation

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

This command attempts to create an IPvlan network, which is a type of network driver for Docker. The operation fails on WSL2, indicating the 'ipvlan' feature is not supported.

```bash
docker network create -d ipvlan \
  --subnet=172.27.160.0/20 \
  --gateway=172.27.160.1 \
  -o parent=eth0 \
  damru-ipvlan
```

--------------------------------

### Apply Damru WSL Fixes and Check Environment

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/damru/wsl_kernel/README.md

After shutting down WSL, these commands are used to apply fixes and check the environment for Damru.

```bash
python -m damru fix-wsl
```

```bash
python -m damru check-env --viewer
```

--------------------------------

### Mount Binderfs for Redroid

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Creates the binderfs directory and mounts the binderfs filesystem, which is required for Android inside Docker. This mount is not persistent across reboots by default.

```bash
sudo mkdir -p /dev/binderfs
sudo mount -t binder binder /dev/binderfs
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Chrome APK Path

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Set the path to a local Chrome APK if using a raw Redroid instance instead of a baked image.

```python
damru.config.CHROME_APK = "/custom/path/to/chrome.apk"
```

--------------------------------

### Fix WSL Environment and Check

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Runs Damru's repair script for WSL and checks the environment. This command attempts to fix common issues like iptables, module loading, binderfs mounting, and Docker daemon startup.

```bash
python -m damru fix-wsl
python -m damru check-env
```

--------------------------------

### Sync DamruPoolSync Usage

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Illustrates the usage of the synchronous `DamruPoolSync`, suitable for distributed task workers and multi-threading. It shows how to configure per-container proxy rotation.

```python
from damru import DamruPoolSync

# proxies enables per-container proxy rotation
with DamruPoolSync(mode="auto", max_devices=5, proxies=["socks5://..."]) as pool:
    with pool.session() as context:
        page = context.new_page()
        page.goto("https://example.com")
```

--------------------------------

### Check Linux/WSL Netfilter Tables

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Confirms the presence and names of Netfilter tables (nat, filter, mangle) in the kernel.

```bash
cat /proc/net/ip_tables_names
```

--------------------------------

### Set Chrome APK Path

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Define the path to the Chrome APK as a constant. This is useful for setting up the environment before initializing Damru.

```python
CHROME_APK = "/mnt/c/Users/you/Downloads/damru/chrome-apks/145.0.7632.75"
```

--------------------------------

### Inspect iptables and nftables Rulesets

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Examine the current NAT and filter table rules in iptables, and list the active ruleset in nftables.

```bash
cat /proc/net/ip_tables_names
iptables -t nat -L
iptables -t filter -L
nft list ruleset
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Linux/WSL Loaded Modules

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Ensures that necessary networking modules like br_netfilter and iptable_nat are loaded.

```bash
lsmod
```

--------------------------------

### Allowing Physical ADB Devices

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Set the DAMRU_ALLOW_PHYSICAL environment variable to 1 to enable auto-detection of physical-looking USB serials for ADB. Use this only for disposable test devices.

```bash
export DAMRU_ALLOW_PHYSICAL=1
```

--------------------------------

### Manually Extract Chrome APKs

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/chrome-apks/README.md

Commands for manually extracting Chrome APKs to the specified directory. This includes creating the directory, setting ownership, and unzipping the archive.

```bash
sudo mkdir -p /home/damru
sudo chown "$USER:$USER" /home/damru
unzip damru-chrome-apks-latest.zip -d /home/damru/chrome-apks
find /home/damru/chrome-apks -maxdepth 2 -name '*.apk' | head
```

--------------------------------

### Connect via ADB

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Connect to the running Damru Android container using ADB and list connected devices to verify the connection. The default connection port is 5555.

```bash
adb connect localhost:5555
adb devices
```

--------------------------------

### DamruPool (Async)

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Manages a pool of asynchronous device sessions, automatically handling Docker container lifecycles and port forwarding. It's designed for massive parallelization.

```APIDOC
## DamruPool (Async)

### Description
Manages a pool of asynchronous device sessions, automatically handling Docker container lifecycles and port forwarding. It's designed for massive parallelization.

### Usage
```python
from damru import DamruPool

# mode="auto" automatically starts and manages Redroid Docker containers
async with DamruPool(mode="auto", max_devices=10) as pool:
    # pool.session() provides an AsyncDamru context from an available container
    async with pool.session() as browser:
        page = await browser.new_page()
        ...
```

### `__init__` Parameters

#### Parameters
- **max_devices** (`int`) - Optional - Number of concurrent instances/containers to maintain. Use `0` only in manual mode to use every detected ADB device. Defaults to config `NUM_DEVICES`.
- **mode** (`str`) - Optional - Deployment mode: `"auto"` manages Redroid via Docker, `"manual"` uses existing ADB devices, and `"mumu"` is experimental. Redroid auto mode is the supported production path. Defaults to config `MODE`.
- **proxy** (`str`) - Optional - One proxy shared by all sessions. Defaults to config `PROXY`.
- **proxies** (`list[str]`) - Optional - Per-worker proxy list. Pool rotates through these by slot index. Defaults to config `PROXIES`.
- **http_proxy** / **http_proxies** (`str` / `list[str]`) - Optional - Android system HTTP proxy override when it differs from the browser proxy. GeoIP is resolved through this path when present. Defaults to config values.
- **device** (`str`) - Optional - Fixed device profile, or `None`/`"random"` for per-session random profiles. Defaults to config `DEVICE`.
- **profile_tier** (`str`) - Optional - Random pool for sessions without a fixed `device`; medium/experimental/all are opt-in. Defaults to config `PROFILE_TIER` / `"premium"`.
- **timezone** / **locale** (`str`) - Optional - Force timezone and locale instead of deriving them from proxy/profile data. Defaults to config values.
- **chrome_apk** (`str`) - Optional - Chrome APK file or split-APK directory for raw/unbaked auto mode. Leave unset to auto-search the validated APK bundle and allow Chrome rotation during random profile actions. Defaults to config `CHROME_APK`.
- **wsl_distro** (`str`) - Optional - Windows only: WSL distro that owns Docker/Redroid. Defaults to config `WSL_DISTRO`.
```

--------------------------------

### Open Watch-Only Viewer

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Opens a live viewer for a Redroid worker in watch-only mode, disabling keyboard, mouse, and touch input.

```bash
python -m damru view --serial wsl:127.0.0.1:5600 --no-control
```

--------------------------------

### Check WSL Kernel Version and iptable_nat Module

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Automation frameworks should query the kernel version and check for the presence of the iptable_nat module. This helps in providing specific error messages if the environment is not correctly configured.

```bash
uname -a
lsmod | grep iptable_nat
```

--------------------------------

### Set Base ADB Port for Testing

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Sets a custom base port for Redroid workers, useful for temporary side-by-side testing of multiple Damru WSL distros.

```powershell
$env:DAMRU_REDROID_BASE_PORT = "5700"
```

--------------------------------

### Force Profile for Custom Android App with Proxy

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Applies hardening to a custom Android app embedding the system WebView, using a proxy. This command avoids Chrome-specific preferences and configures network settings.

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device pixel_8_pro --no-chrome --proxy socks5://user:pass@host:port
```

--------------------------------

### Handle Damru Initialization Errors

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Catch DamruError to handle specific failures during Damru initialization, such as ADB connection or binary patching issues.

```python
from damru import Damru, DamruError

try:
    with Damru() as browser:
        ...
except DamruError as e:
    print(f"Damru failed to initialize: {e}")
```

--------------------------------

### Handle Proxies with Authentication

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Pass authenticated proxies directly to AsyncDamru. For Android system proxy compatibility, provide an HTTP CONNECT proxy separately.

```python
# Pass authenticated proxies directly to AsyncDamru
async with AsyncDamru(proxy="socks5://user:pass@host:port") as browser:
    ...
```

```python
async with AsyncDamru(
    proxy="socks5://user:pass@host:port",
    http_proxy="127.0.0.1:18888",
) as browser:
    page = await browser.new_page()
    await page.goto("https://demo.fingerprint.com/playground")
```

--------------------------------

### Map Host Ports to Container ADB Port

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

This is the standard pattern on Linux for exposing multiple containers with ADB services. It maps different host ports to the container's default ADB port (5555). This is a workaround for scenarios where host networking is used and only one container can bind to the default port.

```bash
-p 5555:5555
-p 5556:5555
```

--------------------------------

### Inspect Hyper-V Virtual Switch Extensions

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Examines Hyper-V virtual switch extensions, particularly those associated with WSL, to debug network configurations in a virtualized environment.

```powershell
Get-VMSwitch
Get-VMSwitchExtension -VMSwitch (Get-VMSwitch | Where-Object {$_.Name -like '*WSL*'}) | Select-Object Name, Enabled
```

--------------------------------

### View Windows NAT Mappings for WSL

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Displays Hyper-V virtual switch NAT translations that map to the WSL subsystem.

```powershell
Get-NetNat
```

--------------------------------

### Run Redroid with Host GPU Passthrough

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/android-virtualization-alternatives.md

This command attempts to pass through the host's GPU to the Redroid container using Docker. It requires the host's DRI devices and video group to be available within the container. Use this when direct hardware access is desired, but be aware it exposes the host's GPU identity.

```bash
docker run --device=/dev/dri --group-add video \
  -v /tmp/.X11-unix:/tmp/.X11-unix \
  redroid/redroid:14.0.0_64only-latest \
  androidboot.redroid_gpu_mode=host
```

--------------------------------

### Inspect Windows Network Configuration

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Examine Windows network configurations including NAT, network interfaces, and firewall profiles relevant to WSL networking.

```powershell
Get-NetNat
Get-NetAdapter
Get-NetIPInterface
Get-NetFirewallProfile
```

--------------------------------

### Check Kernel Configuration for Netfilter and Bridge Modules

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Verifies if the Linux kernel is configured with necessary Netfilter, IP tables, NAT, and bridge modules, which are crucial for Docker networking.

```bash
uname -a
zcat /proc/config.gz | egrep 'NETFILTER|IP_NF|NF_NAT|XT_ADDRTYPE|BRIDGE'
```

--------------------------------

### Check Existing IP Tables Rulesets

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Displays the current filter and NAT rules configured in the IP tables, useful for diagnosing network filtering and routing issues.

```bash
cat /proc/net/ip_tables_names
sudo iptables -t filter -L -n -v
sudo iptables -t nat -L -n -v
```

--------------------------------

### Repair WSL Networking

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/AUTOMATION_GAPS_PLAN.md

Repairs safe Docker, binderfs, iptables, DNS, and WSL networking issues.

```bash
python -m damru fix-wsl
```

--------------------------------

### DamruPoolSync (Sync)

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

The synchronous counterpart to DamruPool, suitable for distributed task workers and multi-threading frameworks. It also supports per-container proxy rotation.

```APIDOC
## DamruPoolSync (Sync)

### Description
The synchronous counterpart to DamruPool, suitable for distributed task workers and multi-threading frameworks. It also supports per-container proxy rotation.

### Usage
```python
from damru import DamruPoolSync

# proxies enables per-container proxy rotation
with DamruPoolSync(mode="auto", max_devices=5, proxies=["socks5://..."]) as pool:
    with pool.session() as context:
        page = context.new_page()
        page.goto("https://example.com")
```
```

--------------------------------

### Open Live Viewer for a Worker

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Opens a live viewer for a specific Redroid worker using its serial number. If omitted, Damru uses the first online ADB device.

```bash
python -m damru view --serial wsl:127.0.0.1:5600
```

--------------------------------

### View Windows Network Address Translation (NAT) and Interfaces

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Inspects Windows NAT configurations and network interfaces, which are part of the networking stack that WSL utilizes.

```powershell
Get-NetNat
Get-NetAdapter
Get-NetIPInterface
```

--------------------------------

### Capture Screenshot

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/VIEWER.md

Captures a screenshot of the Android device display and saves it to a file. Requires specifying the device serial and an output file path.

```bash
python -m damru screenshot --serial wsl:127.0.0.1:5600 --output screen.png
```

--------------------------------

### Test Redroid Binding with Docker

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Validates Docker's ability to map a host port via NAT for Redroid container.

```bash
docker run --rm -p 5600:5555 --privileged damru-redroid:latest
```

--------------------------------

### Record Device Screen

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/VIEWER.md

Records a video of the Android device screen for a specified duration and saves it as an MP4 file. The `--time-limit` option controls the recording length.

```bash
python -m damru record --serial wsl:127.0.0.1:5600 --time-limit 30 --output clip.mp4
```

--------------------------------

### Synchronous Usage with Damru

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Provides a synchronous wrapper for Damru, allowing for blocking operations. Useful for simpler scripts or when async is not required.

```python
from damru import Damru

def run_sync():
    with Damru(device="pixel_8_pro") as browser:
        page = browser.new_page()
        page.goto("https://bot.sannysoft.com/")
        page.wait_for_timeout(5000)
        page.screenshot(path="sannysoft.png")
        print("Passed Sannysoft!")

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run_sync()
```

--------------------------------

### Monitor Low-Level Spoofing Events

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Enable debug mode in Damru to view exact root commands and CDP overrides for monitoring low-level spoofing events.

```python
# Enable debug mode to see exact root commands and CDP overrides
async with AsyncDamru(debug=True) as browser:
    ...
```

--------------------------------

### Prevent Unsupported Multi-Worker Host Networking

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Automation frameworks must prevent the launch of multiple workers using host networking to avoid port collisions and panics. This involves parsing orchestration configurations.

```bash
# Example logic to check orchestration configuration
if [[ "$ORCHESTRATION_CONFIG" == *"--network host"* && $NUM_WORKERS -gt 1 ]]; then
  echo "Error: Multiple workers with --network host is not supported."
  exit 1
fi
```

--------------------------------

### Docker Daemon with Legacy Iptables Backend Failure

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Switching to the legacy iptables backend and restarting the Docker daemon also fails, indicating issues with the 'nat' table's existence or accessibility.

```bash
update-alternatives --set iptables /usr/sbin/iptables-legacy
update-alternatives --set ip6tables /usr/sbin/ip6tables-legacy
dockerd --host=unix:///var/run/docker.sock
```

--------------------------------

### Force Device Profile via Python API

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Programmatically force a device profile using the `force_device_profile` async helper. This function allows setting timezone and locale for the specified device connection.

```python
from damru import force_device_profile

result = await force_device_profile(
    "127.0.0.1:5600",
    "pixel_8_pro",
    timezone="America/New_York",
    locale="en-US",
)
print(result.description)
```

--------------------------------

### Run Damru Functional Test Suite

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Execute the comprehensive functional test suite for Damru. This suite includes various tests to ensure the stealth functionalities are working as expected.

```python
python example.py
```

--------------------------------

### Attempt to Load Common Docker Netfilter Modules

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Attempts to load essential Netfilter modules required for Docker's networking functionalities, such as address type checking, IP tables, NAT, and bridging.

```bash
sudo modprobe xt_addrtype
sudo modprobe ip_tables
sudo modprobe iptable_nat
sudo modprobe iptable_filter
sudo modprobe br_netfilter
```

--------------------------------

### Check NAT Support in WSL Environment

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

A script snippet to check if the current environment supports iptables NAT functionality. It redirects output to /dev/null and checks the exit status to determine NAT support.

```bash
if ! iptables -t nat -L -n >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    # mark environment as no-NAT
fi
```

--------------------------------

### AsyncDamru

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

The primary asynchronous context manager for Damru. It orchestrates stealth layers and returns a Playwright BrowserContext. It supports various initialization parameters for device, proxy, and configuration.

```APIDOC
## `AsyncDamru` (Recommended)

The primary asynchronous context manager. It orchestrates the 8 layers of stealth and returns a Playwright `BrowserContext`.

### Usage
```python
from damru import AsyncDamru

async with AsyncDamru(device="pixel_8_pro", proxy="socks5://...") as context:
    page = await context.new_page()
    await page.goto("https://creepjs.com")
```

### `__init__` Parameters
| Parameter | Type | Default | Description |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| `device` | `str` | `"random"` | Target device string (e.g., `"samsung_galaxy_s24_ultra"`). |
| `profile_tier` | `str` | config `PROFILE_TIER` / `"premium"` | Random profile pool when `device` is unset or `"random"`: `premium`, `premium_verified`, `premium_new`, `medium`, `experimental`, `extended`, or `all`. Explicit named devices ignore this filter. |
| `serial` | `str` | `None` | ADB identifier. If `None`, Damru auto-detects virtual devices only: TCP Redroid endpoints first, then `emulator-*`. Physical-looking USB serials are refused by default. |
| `proxy` | `str` | `None` | Browser proxy URL used for GeoIP and Python-side proxy checks. SOCKS5 and HTTP URLs are accepted. |
| `http_proxy` | `str` | `None` | Android system HTTP proxy as `host:port` or `http://user:pass@host:port`. Use this when Android Chrome must route through an HTTP CONNECT proxy or local bridge. |
| `timezone` | `str` | `None` | Force a specific IANA timezone. Leave unset so Damru resolves it from the active proxy exit. |
| `locale` | `str` | `None` | Force a specific BCP-47 locale. Leave unset so Damru chooses a realistic locale for the proxy country. |
| `debug` | `bool` | `False` | Enables verbose console logging for debugging OS patches. |

### Methods & Properties
*   **`await new_page()`**: Creates a new stealth-hardened `Page`.
*   **`browser`**: Access the underlying Playwright `Browser` object.
*   **`pages`**: List of currently open `Page` objects.
*   **`profile`**: Returns the active `DamruProfile` (Device specs, UA, etc.).
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Chrome APK Path in Python

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Manually sets the path to the Chrome APK directory in Python code. This is used when automatic detection fails or a specific version is required.

```python
CHROME_APK = "/home/damru/chrome-apks/145.0.7632.75"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Docker Data Root in WSL2

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Steps to change the Docker data root directory to a secondary HDD within WSL2 to prevent filling up the primary SSD. This involves stopping Docker, moving data, creating a symlink, and restarting Docker.

```bash
wsl -d Ubuntu
sudo service docker stop
sudo mv /var/lib/docker /mnt/d/docker-data
sudo ln -s /mnt/d/docker-data /var/lib/docker
sudo service docker start
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Windows WSL Version and Status

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Confirms the WSL version and kernel version, ensuring it is operational.

```powershell
wsl --version
wsl --status
```

--------------------------------

### List ADB Devices

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Lists available ADB devices, which is useful for identifying the serial number of your Redroid worker.

```bash
python -m damru devices
```
