--------------------------------

### Force Device Profile via CLI

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Use the CLI to force a specific device profile onto a rooted worker. This command can be used with various options to customize the profile, such as specifying the device, disabling Chrome, clearing the proxy, or setting locale and timezone.

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device pixel_8_pro
```

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device "Xiaomi POCO F5"
```

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device xiaomi_redmi_9a --no-chrome --clear-proxy
```

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device xiaomi_redmi_9a --browser-package org.chromium.webview_shell --locale pt-BR --timezone America/Sao_Paulo
```

--------------------------------

### Open URL in Android Chrome via Damru

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PROOF.md

Opens a specified URL in the Android Chrome browser on a Damru worker.

```powershell
python -m damru open-url --serial 127.0.0.1:5900 --url https://example.com
```

--------------------------------

### Target Non-Default WSL Distro

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Configures Damru to target a specific WSL distribution by setting the DAMRU_WSL_DISTRO environment variable. Useful for testing or managing multiple distros.

```powershell
$env:DAMRU_WSL_DISTRO = "DamruFreshKernelTest"
python -m damru check-env --viewer
python -m damru fix-wsl
```

--------------------------------

### Common Docker Bridge/NAT Failure Message

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

This error indicates that the WSL kernel is missing essential modules for Docker's bridge/NAT functionality.

```text
modprobe: FATAL: Module xt_addrtype not found in directory /lib/modules/...
```

--------------------------------

### Set Chrome APK Path Environment Variable

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/chrome-apks/README.md

Configure the CHROME_APK environment variable to point to a specific Chrome split-APK version directory. This is used when auto-detection fails.

```python
CHROME_APK = "/home/damru/chrome-apks/148.0.7778.217"
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Linux/WSL System Identity

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Verifies the Linux kernel version and identifies the Microsoft kernel in WSL2.

```bash
uname -a
```

--------------------------------

### Force Profile Hardening without Chrome Preferences

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/chrome-apks/README.md

Apply profile hardening to a custom Android WebView. This command is used for apps embedding system WebView, focusing on Android-level hardening without modifying Chrome preferences.

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device pixel_8_pro --no-chrome
```

--------------------------------

### Check iptables Rules in Android Shell

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Lists IP tables rules within an Android environment (Redroid) to check for network filtering and NAT configurations.

```bash
su 0 iptables -L -n
su 0 iptables -t filter -L -n
su 0 iptables -t nat -L -n
```

--------------------------------

### Async Damru with Authenticated Proxy and HTTP Bridge

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Configures Damru for asynchronous use with an authenticated SOCKS5 proxy and an Android HTTP bridge. Chrome will use the http_proxy for timezone and locale resolution.

```python
from damru import AsyncDamru

async with AsyncDamru(
    device="pixel_8_pro",
    proxy="socks5://user:pass@proxy.example:824",
    http_proxy="172.17.0.1:18888",
) as browser:
    page = await browser.new_page()
    await page.goto("https://demo.fingerprint.com/playground")
```

--------------------------------

### Force a Device Profile on a Running Worker

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/DEVICE_PROFILES.md

Use the `damru force-profile` command to apply a specific device profile to an already running worker. This command allows targeting by serial number and specifying the device profile by name, model, or slug.

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device xiaomi_redmi_9a
```

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device "Moto G (5S) Plus" --no-chrome --clear-proxy
```

```bash
python -m damru force-profile --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --device pixel_8_pro --browser-package org.chromium.webview_shell
```

--------------------------------

### Add User to Docker Group

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Grant the current user Docker permissions by adding them to the docker group. A WSL restart is required after executing this command.

```bash
sudo usermod -aG docker $USER
```

--------------------------------

### Quick Check of Redroid Worker

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Runs a quick check on a Redroid worker instance identified by its serial port and saves the output to a JSON file.

```bash
python -m damru quick-check --serial 127.0.0.1:5900 --output /tmp/damru-wsl-quick-5900.json
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Windows IP Interfaces for WSL

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Confirms IPv4 and IPv6 routing metrics for the WSL network adapter.

```powershell
Get-NetIPInterface
```

--------------------------------

### Multi-Page Scraping in One Session

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Scrape multiple pages concurrently within a single Damru session. All pages in this context share the same spoofed Device & IP identity.

```python
async with AsyncDamru(device="random") as browser:
    # All pages in this context share the same spoofed Device & IP identity
    page1 = await browser.new_page()
    page2 = await browser.new_page()
    
    await asyncio.gather(
        page1.goto("https://google.com"),
        page2.goto("https://bing.com")
    )
```

--------------------------------

### Check Windows Firewall Profiles

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Ensures the Windows Defender Firewall is not blocking WSL bridge traffic, which can cause ADB connection issues.

```powershell
Get-NetFirewallProfile
```

--------------------------------

### Checking Android Iptables Filter Table

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Inside a Redroid container, this command attempts to list the 'filter' table of iptables. The failure indicates that the 'filter' table is not available within the Android userspace environment.

```bash
su 0 iptables -L OUTPUT -n
```

--------------------------------

### DamruError

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Custom exception class for Damru-specific failures. Catches issues like ADB connection errors, rooting problems, and binary patching failures.

```APIDOC
## `DamruError`

All Damru-specific failures (ADB connection errors, rooting issues, binary patching failures) raise a `DamruError`.

```python
from damru import Damru, DamruError

try:
    with Damru() as browser:
        ...
except DamruError as e:
    print(f"Damru failed to initialize: {e}")
```
```

--------------------------------

### Repair Worker Internet Connectivity

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/AUTOMATION_GAPS_PLAN.md

Repairs DNS/internet state for WSL/Docker/Android, affecting one or all workers.

```bash
python -m damru fix-internet --all
```

--------------------------------

### Damru Image Baking Process

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/README.md

Commands to bake a custom Redroid image using Damru. This process involves downloading APKs, baking the image, and saving it as a tarball.

```bash
python -m damru install-apks --download
```

```bash
python -m damru bake-image --image damru-redroid:latest
```

```bash
docker save damru-redroid:latest -o damru-redroid-latest.tar
```

--------------------------------

### Damru

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

The synchronous (blocking) version of AsyncDamru, suitable for traditional scripts or multi-threaded environments. It offers the same methods and parameters as AsyncDamru but without the need for `await`.

```APIDOC
## `Damru` (Synchronous)

The blocking version of `AsyncDamru`. Perfect for traditional scripts or multi-threaded environments.

### Usage
```python
from damru import Damru

with Damru(device="pixel_7") as context:
    page = context.new_page()
    page.goto("https://bot.sannysoft.com")
```
*Note: Methods and parameters are identical to `AsyncDamru` but without `await`.*
```

--------------------------------

### Verify Windows Network Adapter Status

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Checks the operational status of the virtual network interface used by WSL.

```powershell
Get-NetAdapter
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Docker to Bypass Kernel NAT

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Launch the Docker daemon with specific flags to bypass the Netfilter subsystem and utilize its userland proxy mechanism for port publishing. This is useful when kernel NAT is unavailable or problematic.

```bash
dockerd --iptables=false --ip-masq=false --bridge=none --userland-proxy=true
```

--------------------------------

### Run Redroid Worker in Host Network Mode

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Launches a Redroid worker container using the host network mode, bypassing Docker's network isolation for direct access.

```bash
docker run -itd --rm --privileged \
  --network host \
  -v ~/data-worker1:/data \
  --name redroid-worker1 \
  damru-redroid:latest
```

--------------------------------

### Open URL in a Specific Worker

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/AUTOMATION_GAPS_PLAN.md

Opens a specified URL in the browser of a particular worker. Requires the worker's serial number and the URL.

```bash
python -m damru open-url --serial 127.0.0.1:5600 --url https://example.com
```

--------------------------------

### AndroidDevice Database Access

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Provides access to a database of 155 built-in device profiles, allowing retrieval of physical specifications and selection of profiles by name, model, slug, or random criteria.

```APIDOC
## Device Management

### `AndroidDevice` Database

Access the physical specifications of the 155 built-in device profiles. The full generated list is in [DEVICE_PROFILES.md](DEVICE_PROFILES.md). Profiles can be selected by exact marketing name, model, or slug. Default random selection uses the premium pool only: 51 original verified profiles plus 49 high-confidence new profiles. Medium and experimental profiles remain available by explicit name or opt-in tier.

### Functions

- **list_device_names()**: Returns a list of all available device profile names.
- **get_device(name: str)**: Retrieves the `AndroidDevice` object for a specific device name, model, or slug.
- **get_devices_by_tier(tier: str)**: Returns a list of `AndroidDevice` objects for a specified tier (e.g., 'premium', 'medium', 'experimental').
- **get_random_device(**
    android_version: str = None,
    tier: str = "premium",
    **kwargs
**)**: Selects and returns a random `AndroidDevice` profile, optionally filtered by Android version and tier.

#### Example Usage
```python
from damru import list_device_names, get_device, get_devices_by_tier, get_random_device

# Get hardware specs for a specific phone
pixel = get_device("pixel_8_pro")
print(f"Cores: {pixel.hardware_concurrency}, RAM: {pixel.device_memory}GB")

# Select a random Android 13 profile
random_phone = get_random_device(android_version="13")
```
```

--------------------------------

### Check WSL Kernel Status

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/WSL_KERNEL.md

Use this command to check the status of the WSL kernel without making any changes.

```powershell
python -m damru wsl-kernel status
```

--------------------------------

### Validate Docker Engine Status

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/2.md

Checks the Docker daemon state and warns if essential bridge or NAT features are missing.

```bash
docker info
```

--------------------------------

### Connect ADB to Redroid Worker

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

Connects the Android Debug Bridge (ADB) to a running Redroid worker instance, typically accessed via the WSL IP address.

```bash
# From WSL or Windows using WSL IP:
adb connect <WSL_IP>:5555
```

--------------------------------

### Redroid ADB Port Configuration

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/research/new/You are a senior Linux kernel, Docker networking,.md

These lines in a boot properties file attempt to change the ADB TCP port for a Redroid instance. This is a workaround to allow multiple Redroid instances to be accessible if using host networking.

```properties
service.adb.tcp.port=5556
persist.adb.tcp.port=5556
```

--------------------------------

### Disable Chrome APK Path Specification

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/README.md

Configure Damru to auto-search for Chrome APKs in the project's 'chrome-apks/' directory. When set to None, Damru will rotate APK versions for random profile actions.

```python
# None = auto-searches the 'chrome-apks/' directory in the project root
CHROME_APK = None
```

```python
# Or specify an absolute path:
# CHROME_APK = "/mnt/c/path/to/damru/chrome-apks/145.0.7632.75"
```

--------------------------------

### Bypass CDN TLS with curl_cffi

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/PYTHON_API.md

Defeat CDN TLS and other TLS-fingerprinting WAFs by replaying requests through curl_cffi browser impersonation. This persistent interception is set for a specific domain.

```python
from damru.bypass import arm_bypass_async

async with AsyncDamru() as browser:
    page = await browser.new_page()
    
    # Persistent interception for this domain
    await arm_bypass_async(page, domain="target-site.com")
    
    # All document navigations now use randomized browser TLS hashes
    await page.goto("https://target-site.com")
```

--------------------------------

### Quick Check a Specific Worker

Source: https://github.com/akwin1234/damru/blob/main/docs/AUTOMATION_GAPS_PLAN.md

Performs a quick check on a specific worker identified by its serial number.

```bash
python -m damru quick-check --serial 127.0.0.1:5600
```

=== COMPLETE CONTENT === This response contains all available snippets from this library. No additional content exists. Do not make further requests.
